name: HMS Lady Philomena (FY 148)
events: This convoy departed Methil around 1330 B/13.
It was made up of the following merchant vessels; Agatha (Dutch (tanker), 3369 GRT, built 1927) and Don (British, 1037 GRT, built 1924).
The submarine HMS P 51 (Lt. M.L.C. Crawford, DSC, RN) and the motor minesweeper HM MMS 191 (T/Lt. H.A.G. Ceulemans, RNR) were also part of the convoy.

On departure from Methil the convoy was escorted by the escort destroyers HMS Liddesdale (Lt.Cdr. A.A. Mackenzie, RNR) and HMS Lauderdale (Lt. G.D. Pound, DSC, RN). The A/S trawler HMS Berkshire (T/Skr. W. Patterson, RNR) was also with the convoy with the task to provide close escort for HMS P 51.
Off 22 buoy a merchant vessel joined the convoy. This was the Lochside II (British, 368 GRT, built 1925). She was later detached to the Tyne. [This ship is not mentioned in the Commodore report.]

From Blyth the following merchant vessels joined the convoy; Betswood (British, 1051 GRT, built 1936), Corglen (British, 2822 GRT, built 1928), Corstar (British, 2337 GRT, built 1918), Corundum (British, 929 GRT, 1925), Crackshot (British, 2379 GRT, built 1924), Hoogland (Dutch, 1298 GRT, built 1916) and Vilk (British (former Estonian), 1933 GRT, built 1913).
At 0830 B/14, the convoy overtook and merged with the Tyne section of the convoy off 18C buoy.

The Tyne section of the convoy was made up of the following merchant vessels; Bramhill (British, 1821 GRT, built 1923), Bruce M. (British, 1887 GRT, built 1927), Fulham VI (British, 1552, built 1941) and Stanhope (British, 2337 GRT, built 1919).
The Tyne section was escorted by the auxiliary AA vessel HMS Mona's Isle (Cdr.(Retd.) J.H. Blair, DSO, RD, RNR) which returned to the Tyne after the convoy sections had merged.

From Sunderland the following merchant vessels joined the convoy; Capitol (British, 1558 GRT, built 1941), Cerne (British, 1242 GRT, built 1924), Corfirth (British, 1803 GRT, built 1934), Ewell (British, 1334 GRT, built 1926), Ightham (British, 1337 GRT, built 1914), Penshurst (British, 1454 GRT, built 1921), Redcar (British, 1475 GRT, built 1920), Redriff (British, 1547 GRT, built 1925) and Sylvia Beale (British, 1040 GRT, built 1938).

According to the convoy escort report a merchant vessel joined the convoy coming from the Humber. This was the tanker San Roberto (British (tanker), 5890 GRT, built 1922) which passed Spurn Head on 14 August and arrived at Harwich on 15 August. [This ship is not mentioned in the Commodore report.]
At 1440 B/14, off 62D buoy, the A/S trawler HMS Lady Philomena (Skr. A. Robinson, RNR) took over from HMS Berkshire as close escort for HMS P 51.
At 1700 B/14, off H3 buoy, the patrol vessel HMS Mallard (Lt.Cdr. D. Parsons, RD, RNR) and two ML's, HM ML 204 (T/Lt. G.C. Davey, RNVR) and HM 299 (Lt. D.A.P. Smith, RANVR) joined the convoy escort.

According to the convoy escort report a merchant vessel joined the convoy coming from Harwich, this was the Southwick (British, 443 GRT, built 1917) and one merchant vessel was detached to Harwich, the San Roberto). Also HM MMS 191 was detached to Harwich.
The convoy was disbanded off Southend around 1200 A/15. (1)
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
